docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
HashMap is a non-thread-safe key-value data structure that provides O(1) average lookup performance using hashing.

ConcurrentHashMap is a thread-safe implementation designed for concurrent access. It uses fine-grained synchronization and CAS operations, allowing multiple threads to work efficiently.

Hashtable is a legacy synchronized implementation that locks most operations using a single monitor, resulting in lower scalability.

docs.title: Follow-up: How does ConcurrentHashMap achieve thread safety?
docs.text:
ConcurrentHashMap avoids locking the entire map.

It synchronizes only small portions of the internal structure when required and uses CAS operations for simple updates.

This allows multiple threads to update different buckets simultaneously.

docs.title: Follow-up: What is CAS?
docs.text:
CAS stands for Compare-And-Set.

It is a hardware-supported atomic operation provided by the CPU.

Process:

  1. Compare current value with expected value.
  2. If equal, update the value.
  3. Otherwise fail and retry.

AtomicInteger and ConcurrentHashMap use CAS internally.

docs.title: Follow-up: What is a hash collision?
docs.text:
A hash collision occurs when multiple keys map to the same bucket.

HashMap stores multiple entries within that bucket and uses equals() to locate the correct entry.

docs.title: Follow-up: Why is Hashtable slower?
docs.text:
Hashtable synchronizes most operations on a single lock.

This forces threads to wait even when accessing unrelated entries.

ConcurrentHashMap provides much better scalability.

docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
equals() defines logical equality.

hashCode() determines the bucket location used by hash-based collections.

Whenever equals() is overridden, hashCode() must also be overridden.

docs.title: Why?
docs.text:
HashMap first uses hashCode() to find a bucket and then uses equals() to find the matching object.

Failing to override both methods can cause lookup failures.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Two objects may be logically equal:

user1.equals(user2) == true

but produce different hash codes, preventing HashMap from locating them correctly.

docs.title: Common Follow-Up Questions
docs.text:
• What is CAS?
  • How does ConcurrentHashMap work internally?
  • What is fine-grained locking?

docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
Heap stores objects and arrays and is shared among all threads.

Stack stores method frames, local variables, and references and is private to each thread.

Metaspace stores class metadata and resides in native memory outside the heap.

docs.title: Heap
docs.text:
Stores:

  • Objects
  • Arrays

Managed by the garbage collector.

Shared between all threads.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
Stores:

  • Method calls
  • Local variables
  • References

Each thread has its own stack.

Follows LIFO behavior.

docs.title: Why are local variables thread-safe?
docs.text:
Each thread owns its own stack.

Local variables are not shared between threads.

docs.title: Metaspace
docs.text:
Stores:

  • Class definitions
  • Methods
  • Fields
  • Annotations
  • Bytecode metadata

docs.title: Is Metaspace part of the heap?
docs.text:
No.

Metaspace uses native memory outside the Java heap.

docs.title: What existed before Metaspace?
docs.text:
PermGen.

Java 8 replaced PermGen with Metaspace.

docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
The String Pool is a special memory area used to store String literals.

If a String already exists in the pool, the JVM reuses it instead of creating a new object.

docs.title: Where is String Pool stored?
docs.text: In modern Java versions, the String Pool resides inside the heap.

docs.title: Why is String immutable?
docs.text:
• Thread safety
  • String pooling
  • Hashcode caching
  • Security

docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
Full GC performs garbage collection across the entire heap.

Application threads are paused during the operation.

Frequent Full GC events usually indicate memory pressure or memory leaks.

docs.title: What is Stop-The-World?
docs.text: Application threads are temporarily paused while garbage collection is performed.

docs.title: Interview Answer
docs.text:
ClassLoaders load classes into memory at runtime.

Main ClassLoaders:

  • Bootstrap ClassLoader
  • Platform ClassLoader
  • Application ClassLoader

docs.title: Parent Delegation Model
docs.text:
A child class loader asks its parent to load a class first.

This prevents duplicate loading and improves security.

docs.title: What loads java.lang.String?
docs.text: Bootstrap ClassLoader.
